steps.title: Solve the linear equation
steps.content:
Starting with $10 = 2x + 4$, subtract 4 from both sides:
$$10 - 4 = 2x + 4 - 4$$
$$6 = 2x$$
Divide both sides by 2:
$$\frac{6}{2} = \frac{2x}{2}$$
$$x = 3$$

steps.title: Count the solutions
steps.content: We found exactly one value of $x$ that satisfies the equation. Since a linear equation in one variable with a non-zero coefficient on the variable produces exactly one solution, there is exactly one solution to this equation. That matches choice B — B is correct.
